content: Ali had 120 stickers. He gave 1/4 of them to Ben and 1/3 of the remainder to Cindy. How many stickers did Cindy receive?
A fruit seller had 240 apples. He sold 3/8 of them on Monday. He sold 1/4 of the remainder on Tuesday. How many apples did he have left?
The ratio of boys to girls in a class is 3:5. If there are 24 boys, how many students are there altogether?
The ratio of Tom's money to Jerry's money is 4:7. If Jerry has $35, how much money do they have altogether?
The ratio of the number of red beads to blue beads is 2:5. If there are 30 more blue beads than red beads, how many beads are there in total?
The average of 5 numbers is 36. When a 6th number is added, the average becomes 40. What is the 6th number?
The average mass of 4 boys is 42 kg. The average mass of 6 girls is 38 kg. What is the average mass of all 10 children?
The average of 3 numbers is 25. Two of the numbers are 18 and 29. What is the third number?
If x + 7 = 15, find the value of x.
Mary has x stickers. She buys 12 more stickers. She then gives away 5 stickers. Express the number of stickers she has now in terms of x.
The cost of 3 pens and 2 erasers is $9.50. Each eraser costs $0.80. What is the cost of each pen?
Tom has x marbles. Jerry has 3 times as many marbles as Tom. Together, they have 48 marbles. Find the value of x.
A shop sold 150 items in 3 days. It sold twice as many items on Day 2 as on Day 1. It sold 10 more items on Day 3 than on Day 2. How many items were sold on Day 1?
Mr Tan gave 2/5 of his money to his wife and spent 1/3 of the remainder on a watch. If he had $240 left, how much money did he have at first?
The ratio of the number of stamps John had to the number Peter had was 5:3. After John gave 20 stamps to Peter, they had the same number of stamps. How many stamps did John have at first?
A rectangle has a length of 12 cm and a breadth of 8 cm. Find the area and perimeter of the rectangle.
A tank is 3/4 filled with water. When 15 litres of water are added, the tank becomes completely full. What is the capacity of the tank?
Express 3y + 2y - y + 4 in the simplest form.
The ratio of the number of fiction books to non-fiction books in a library is 7:4. There are 84 fiction books. How many non-fiction books are there?
Amy, Ben, and Clara share $180 in the ratio 2:3:4. How much does each person get?
The average height of 8 students is 142 cm. When 2 more students join the group, the average height becomes 140 cm. What is the average height of the 2 new students?
Peter had 3 times as many cards as Paul. After Peter gave 26 cards to Paul, they each had an equal number of cards. How many cards did Peter have at first?
A piece of wire 56 cm long is bent to form a square. What is the length of each side of the square? What is the area of the square?
Mrs Lee bought 5 kg of rice at $2.30 per kg and 3 packets of sugar at $1.50 each. How much did she spend in total?
The ratio of Siti's age to Mei's age is 3:5. Siti is 6 years younger than Mei. Find Mei's age.
A piece of rope is 3/7 m long. Ahmad cuts off 1/4 of it. What is the length of the rope he cut off?
If 2x + 5 = 19, find the value of 3x - 2.
The sum of three consecutive numbers is 96. Find the three numbers.
Ravi had some $2 notes and $5 notes. He had 15 notes altogether and the total value was $48. How many $2 notes did he have?
A farmer has chickens and cows. He counts 50 heads and 140 legs. How many chickens and how many cows does he have?
The ratio of water in Tank A to Tank B is 5:3. Tank A has 40 litres more water than Tank B. How much water is in each tank?
A baker made 360 muffins. He sold 5/8 of them in the morning and 1/3 of the remainder in the afternoon. How many muffins were left?
There are 3 more girls than boys in a class. The ratio of boys to girls is 5:6. How many children are there in the class?
The average score of a class of 30 students in a math test is 72. The average score of the boys is 68 and the average score of the girls is 78. How many boys are there in the class?
Find the value of 4a - 3b when a = 5 and b = 2.
A square has a perimeter of 48 cm. Find the area of the square.
The total cost of 4 identical books and a $3 ruler is $27. What is the cost of one book?
Jane is x years old. Her mother is 4 times as old as Jane. The sum of their ages is 60. Find Jane's age.
The ratio of boys to girls at a concert is 3:7. There are 200 people at the concert. How many more girls than boys are there?
Ali saved $5 every week. Bala saved $8 every week. After how many weeks would Bala have saved $36 more than Ali?
